name: Frozen Sweet Potato Pudding
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Dark rum or Mead-sweet Sherry
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil sweet potatoes and a pinch of salt in enough water to cover until very tender, about 30 minutes. Drain and cool for easy handling; peel.
2. In a heavy saucepan, combine the sugar, lime zest, and water. Heat over medium heat until the sugar dissolves completely, approximately 5 minutes. Remove from heat and discard the zest.
3. In the top of a double boiler set over gently simmering water, whisk together egg whites, remaining sugar, 1 tablespoon water, and cream of tartar until heated to 160°F (70°C). Be careful not to allow the mixture to become too thick; it should be white and foamy. Transfer to a bowl and let cool slightly.
4. In a food processor, pulse sweet potatoes with the cooled syrup until smooth. Pour this mixture into the double boiler over the simmering water. Stir in egg yolks, rum (or sherry), mace, ginger, and bitters until combined. Cook until the mixture thickens, about 10 minutes.
5. Beat egg whites until stiff peaks form; they should be glossy and hold their shape. Gently fold one-third of the whites into the sweet potato mixture, then add the remaining whites in thirds, making sure not to deflate them. Cover tightly and refrigerate until firm, at least 2 hours.
6. To serve, remove from the refrigerator and allow it to soften slightly before serving.
